title: Quiz: Environment Engineering
description: Test your understanding of dissipative state preparation and reservoir engineering

questions.question: What is the key condition for a pure state |psi_T> to be the steady state of Lindblad dynamics?

options.text: |psi_T> must be an eigenstate of sigma_z

questions.explanation: For a pure state |psi_T> to be a steady state, it must be in the kernel of all Lindblad operators: L_k|psi_T> = 0 for all k. This ensures the dissipators don't affect the target state.

questions.question: What determines the convergence rate to the steady state in Lindblad dynamics?

options.text: The dimension of the Hilbert space
The spectral gap of the Lindbladian
The purity of the initial state

questions.explanation: The spectral gap (the smallest non-zero eigenvalue magnitude of the Lindbladian) determines how fast the system approaches steady state: ||rho(t) - rho_ss|| ~ exp(-gap * t).

questions.question: In autonomous quantum error correction, how are errors corrected?

options.text: By measuring syndromes and applying corrections
By engineered dissipation that returns the system to the code space

questions.explanation: Autonomous error correction uses engineered Lindblad operators that pump error states back to the code space continuously, without requiring measurement.

questions.question: Dissipative state preparation can prepare entangled states that would be impossible to reach from a product state using only unitary evolution.
questions.explanation: False. Unitary evolution can reach any pure state from any other pure state. However, dissipative preparation can be more robust and doesn't require precise timing control.

questions.question: What is an advantage of dissipative state preparation over unitary preparation?

options.text: It is robust to initial state and perturbations

questions.explanation: Dissipative preparation is robust: the target state is reached regardless of the initial state, and small perturbations are continuously corrected by the dissipation.

questions.question: To prepare the singlet state (|01> - |10>)/sqrt(2), the Lindblad operators should pump population from:

options.text: The triplet states to the singlet

questions.explanation: To prepare the singlet as steady state, the Lindblad operators should be L = |singlet><triplet_k| which pump population from the triplet manifold to the singlet.

questions.question: Which are valid applications of environment engineering? (Select all)
questions.explanation: All options are valid applications of environment engineering: dissipative entanglement generation, autonomous error correction, cooling to ground states, and dissipative gate implementation.

questions.question: If the spectral gap of a Lindbladian is 0.1, approximately how long does it take to reach 99% of the steady state?
questions.explanation: Time to 99% fidelity ~ -ln(0.01)/gap = 4.6/0.1 = 46 time units. The decay is exponential: error ~ exp(-gap * t), so t ~ ln(100)/gap for 99%.
